mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with support for various hardware and models. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and potential complexity in setup for distributed inference.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This adds the vLLM Helm repository.

steps.title: Install vLLM CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for vLLM to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This installs the necessary CRDs for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace, ensuring that the correct version is used.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> This installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource for vLLM, which defines the model and configuration for serving.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-2'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This creates an example vLLM resource using the GPT-2 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-2'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM Custom Resource
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This verifies that the vLLM instance is running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the vLLM Custom Resource and any associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete crd vllms.vllm.io

```

> This uninstalls the vLLM Helm release.
> This deletes the vLLM CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This process will upgrade vLLM to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Backup the current vLLM configuration.
> This upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM instance is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Custom Resource may not be installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the vLLM Custom Resource is created and check the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not be reachable or the kubeconfig is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and ensure your Kubernetes cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or the image name is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the container image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM application may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the vLLM pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust the configuration or resource limits.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models, improved memory management, and enhanced performance optimizations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM instance listed with the command 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm', showing the status as 'Running'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources and the necessary tools installed.
